 {"id":"bd-g28e","title":"Execute stdlib_sort fixture through isolated harness","description":"Upgrade stdlib_sort conformance from schema-only validation to per-case isolated harness execution, plus top-level fixture metadata assertions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-16T23:47:59.748287795Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-16T23:51:01.287010754Z","closed_at":"2026-04-16T23:51:01.284058581Z","close_reason":"Upgraded stdlib_sort conformance coverage to execute each fixture case through the isolated harness subprocess path and added fixture metadata assertions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-g7ux","title":"inet_addr returns host-order u32 instead of network-order","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T10:10:37.756487821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:27:27.384420744Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T17:27:27.384352917Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","inet"],"comments":[{"id":1086,"issue_id":"bd-g7ux","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"frankenlibc_core::inet::inet_addr uses u32::from_be_bytes(octets) which returns the host-endian integer value (0x7F000001 = 2130706433 for 127.0.0.1). glibc inet_addr returns the same 4 bytes stored as a native u32 in network byte order (0x0100007F = 16777343 on LE). Fix: change from_be_bytes to from_ne_bytes in crates/frankenlibc-core/src/inet/mod.rs:63. Detected by conformance differential execution.","created_at":"2026-02-13T10:10:45Z"},{"id":1087,"issue_id":"bd-g7ux","author":"PurpleHawk","text":"Fixed inet_addr to return network byte order. Changed u32::from_be_bytes to u32::from_ne_bytes in frankenlibc-core/src/inet/mod.rs:63. Updated 5 tests that incorrectly used to_be_bytes() to use to_ne_bytes(). Also fixed pre-existing pthread compilation error: added tls_values field to ThreadHandle and wired up register_thread_tls call with the correct 2-arg signature. All 61 inet tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:27:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-g87a","title":"syscall: timer_gettime valid timer with null output should return EFAULT exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EFAULT for timer_gettime(valid_timer, NULL). Add focused ABI regression coverage so the valid-timer null-output contract is frozen exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:44:34.401094428Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:46:21.663870926Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T08:46:21.661719814Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity timer_gettime(valid_timer, NULL) behavior: exact EFAULT and new ABI regression coverage; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-gay9","title":"syscall: sched_getparam negative pid with null output should still return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for sched_getparam(-1, NULL), so the invalid-pid contract dominates the null output pointer. Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:55:23.152293795Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:55:23.152293795Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gcy","title":"bd-c1x subtask: Futex condvar core implementation (wait/signal/broadcast/timedwait)","description":"Background:
- Condvar implementation must coordinate with mutex ownership and futex wake ordering safely.

Goal:
- Implement futex-based condvar core with deterministic sequence and wake semantics.

Deliverables:
1) Internal condvar sequence/counter design.
2) wait path with unlock/relock choreography around futex_wait.
3) signal/broadcast wake policy.
4) Timed wait with absolute deadline handling.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Core condvar operations satisfy spec for success/error/timeout cases.
- No missed wakeups in deterministic stress tests.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests: signal-before-wait, wait-before-signal, broadcast-many, timeout races.
- Structured logs: trace_id, mode, condvar_state, mutex_state, wake_count, timeout_state, errno.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleHawk","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:51.531693883Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:32.207008001Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:32.206927771Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","futex","implementation","pthread","testing"],"comments":[{"id":1088,"issue_id":"bd-gcy","author":"PurpleHawk","text":"Completed futex condvar core implementation (bd-gcy). Deliverables:

1) CondvarData repr(C) struct with seq counter, waiter count, associated mutex, and clock_id — overlays onto pthread_cond_t memory (20 bytes, fits in 48-byte struct).

2) condvar_wait(): atomic unlock-wait-relock choreography using sys_futex. Captures seq before unlock, parks with FUTEX_WAIT on seq word, relocks via CAS+futex loop on return. Validates mutex association invariant (EINVAL on mismatch).

3) condvar_timedwait(): uses FUTEX_WAIT_BITSET with absolute timeout. FUTEX_CLOCK_REALTIME flag selects clock domain matching condvar's configured clock. Returns ETIMEDOUT on deadline expiry after relocking mutex (per POSIX).

4) condvar_signal(): increments seq + futex_wake(1). condvar_broadcast(): increments seq + futex_wake(INT_MAX). Both are no-ops when no waiters (seq still incremented for consistency).

5) relock_mutex() helper: simple CAS loop with futex parking for relocking after condvar wait.

6) 64 total tests pass: 41 contract tests (bd-blg) + 23 core implementation tests including 4 threaded integration tests:
   - core_condvar_wait_signal_roundtrip (single waiter + signal)
   - core_condvar_broadcast_wakes_all (4 waiters + broadcast)
   - core_condvar_timedwait_expires (past deadline -> ETIMEDOUT)
   - core_condvar_mutex_association_mismatch (EINVAL on different mutex)

7) Exports from pthread module: CondvarData, condvar_init/destroy/wait/timedwait/signal/broadcast.

Verification: cargo test -p frankenlibc-core -- pthread::cond (64 passed, 0 failed). cargo check --all-targets clean.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-gd30","title":"Conformance: execute termios_ops fixture cases","description":"Add direct frankenlibc_fixture_exec coverage for termios_ops.json cases in the harness.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T06:53:29.353956319Z","created_by":"SilverHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-18T07:11:54.736050203Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T07:11:54.734003312Z","close_reason":"Added direct execute_fixture_case coverage for termios_ops fixtures and fixed executor speed-field setup; verified with rch test/check/clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","termios","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-geoa","title":"resolv: getaddrinfo_a zero-item and null-request-list degenerate calls should succeed","description":"Host glibc returns success with errno unchanged for synchronous-degenerate getaddrinfo_a calls such as nitems=0 (even with a NULL list) and positive-length request arrays whose entries are all NULL. FrankenLibC currently returns EAI_SYSTEM/ENOSYS. Restore those no-op success semantics in unistd_abi.rs and lock them with focused ABI tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:27:52.529653096Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:30:33.157717821Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:30:33.155545909Z","close_reason":"Restored host-parity getaddrinfo_a success semantics for zero-item and all-NULL synchronous-degenerate request sets, with focused ABI regression coverage verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-h5x.2","title":"Pthread primitive closure wave beyond mutex/condvar/thread bootstrap","description":"Background:
- Existing work addresses mutex/thread bootstrap, but remaining primitives can still hide glibc dependence.

Scope:
- Close remaining pthread primitive call-throughs (rwlock, barrier, once, attr/timeouts where in target scope).
- Ensure ABI paths route to internal/raw implementations with deterministic fallback policies.

Deliverables:
1) Primitive-by-primitive replacement plan.
2) Implemented routing updates and conformance fixtures.
3) Branch metrics for contention/wait/timeout behavior.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Target pthread primitives in replacement profile show zero disallowed call-through.
- Strict/hardened tests pass for covered primitives.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for state machines and edge/error paths.
- E2E concurrency fixtures with strict/hardened comparisons.
- Logs: trace_id, primitive, mode, path(internal/raw/fallback), errno, timing.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for happy-path, edge-case, and invalid-input behavior.
- [ ] Property-style or fuzz-seed replay coverage added where state space is large/combinatorial.
- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.
- [ ] Release/merge gate blocked if any required test or log artifact is missing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildCrane","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:26.799566701Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T22:41:54.188485346Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T22:41:54.188197491Z","close_reason":"All pthread primitives have native implementations: mutex (normal/recursive/errorcheck), condvar (wait/signal/broadcast/timedwait/clockwait), rwlock, barrier, spinlock, once, TLS, cancel, naming, getattr_np, tryjoin_np, timedjoin_np, clockjoin_np. 142 exports, 169 tests. Remaining 5 resolve_host_symbol_raw calls are INTENTIONAL interpose fallbacks (host delegation for non-managed threads) — all have working native paths via FORCE_NATIVE_THREADING.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","futex","pthread"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1gh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-c1x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-h5x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-h5x.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-yos","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","depends_on_id":"bd-z84","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1099,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","author":"WildCrane","text":"Progress slice landed: native cooperative cancellation path for pthread cancellation APIs in ABI layer.\n\nImplemented in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:\n- Replaced glibc call-through for pthread_cancel/pthread_setcancelstate/pthread_setcanceltype/pthread_testcancel.\n- Added thread-local cancellation state/type plus pending-request registry.\n- Added unit tests for API input validation and pending-cancel consume semantics.\n\nVerification attempted (all via rch):\n- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check (fails due unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in other files)\n- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets (passed once, later blocked by unrelated membrane compile issue in runtime_math/evidence.rs: missing read_u32)\n- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (fails due broad pre-existing lint debt outside this slice)\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi pthread_cancel -- --nocapture (blocked by same unrelated membrane compile issue)\n\nCoordination:\n- Reserved pthread_abi + related conformance artifacts under reason bd-h5x.2.\n- support_matrix.json remains locked by SwiftLeopard, so matrix classification updates are pending coordination.","created_at":"2026-02-25T04:33:11Z"},{"id":1357,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","author":"ChartreuseCrane","text":"ChartreuseCrane analysis: The 5 remaining pthread host delegations (pthread_getattr_np, pthread_cond_clockwait, pthread_timedjoin_np, pthread_tryjoin_np, pthread_clockjoin_np) are ALL intentional interpose delegations — they only fire for non-managed (glibc-created) objects. For FrankenLibC-managed objects, native futex/syscall paths already work. This is correct LD_PRELOAD behavior. These become dead code in replace mode. See bd-zh1y.1 for tracking.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:57:40Z"},{"id":1369,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","author":"LavenderMarsh","text":"Progress update on pthread attr/cancel cleanup: patched pthread_mutex_init and pthread_cond_init host-delegating paths to reject destroyed attr words (word == 0) and invalid managed attr bitfields before translation. mutex_init_rejects_destroyed_attr, cond_init_rejects_destroyed_attr, and mutex_init_rejects_unsupported_extension_attributes now pass via rch. Follow-up fix in the same lane: converted the test-only FORCE_NATIVE_THREADING override from process-global behavior to a thread-local override with global fallback, then routed pthread ABI delegation checks through force_native_threading_enabled(). This removes cross-test interference in pthread_abi_test, where isolated passes were failing only in the full binary due to concurrent override leakage. Verification so far: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_check_lavendermarsh_pthreadfix cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests PASS, rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_ws_check_lavendermarsh6 cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS, rch exec -- cargo fmt --check PASS. Running remaining evidence now: full pthread_abi_test rerun and workspace clippy.","created_at":"2026-04-03T18:56:33Z"},{"id":1370,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","author":"LavenderMarsh","text":"Additional verification note: workspace clippy finished clean via rch (env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_ws_clippy_lavendermarsh6 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings). A first deterministic pthread_abi_test rerun failed on a remote worker cache issue in the cc crate, not repo code; rerunning with a fresh remote CARGO_HOME now to isolate the actual test binary result from worker state.","created_at":"2026-04-03T18:59:05Z"},{"id":1371,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","author":"LavenderMarsh","text":"Fresh rch rerun with isolated remote CARGO_HOME progressed past compile and is executing pthread_abi_test serially (--test-threads=1). Current live output shows previously flaky cases green in-sequence: cancel_running_thread, cond_clockwait_monotonic_future_signal_returns_zero_on_managed_condvar, mutex_timedlock_future_deadline_times_out_before_unlock, plus the attr rejection tests. Waiting only on final test-result line now.","created_at":"2026-04-03T19:00:20Z"},{"id":1372,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","author":"LavenderMarsh","text":"Found the remaining pthread_abi_test stall: native_condvar_wait_rejects_host_mutex_mismatch was stale and constructed a host condvar + host mutex pair while asserting the native mismatch EINVAL path, so it blocked forever. Patched by adding scoped mutex-force-native test hooks in pthread_abi.rs and rewriting the test to create a native condvar through our ABI while creating the mutex via libc::pthread_mutex_init, which deterministically exercises the intended mismatch case.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:05:29Z"},{"id":1373,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.2","author":"LavenderMarsh","text":"Verification complete. After replacing the stale host/host condvar mismatch fixture with a real native-condvar/host-mutex mismatch fixture, the exact test passes via rch: env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_pthread_single_lavendermarsh9 cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_abi_test native_condvar_wait_rejects_host_mutex_mismatch -- --exact --nocapture. Full serial pthread ABI suite also passes via rch: env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_pthread_full_lavendermarsh10 cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_abi_test -- --nocapture --test-threads=1 => 169 passed, 0 failed. Companion checks remain green: cargo check -p frankenlibc-abi --tests PASS, cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings PASS, cargo fmt --check PASS.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:07:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-h5x.3","title":"Non-threading call-through elimination + enforcement CI gate","description":"Background:
- Non-threading families (stdio/io/dlfcn/startup edges) can still conceal significant call-through debt.

Scope:
- Execute call-through elimination wave for high-traffic non-threading families prioritized by trace frequency and replacement-level necessity.
- Add CI gate that fails on forbidden call-through regressions.

Deliverables:
1) Ranked elimination backlog for non-threading families.
2) Replacement routing updates with fixture coverage.
3) Forbidden-call-through CI checker with clear triage output.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Forbidden call-through set trends to zero for declared level targets.
- Regression attempts fail CI with symbol-level diagnostics.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for checker policy logic.
- E2E smoke verification with replacement profile guard.
- Logs: trace_id, symbol, callthrough_detected, policy_rule, verdict, artifact_ref.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"Session 13 (RusticWolf): nativized 31 more dlsym_passthrough→Implemented in glibc_internal_abi.rs. Batches: printf-ext stubs (8), NSS stubs (7), pthread_cleanup TLS linked-list (4), __clone naked asm (1), misc stubs (3), obstack native (8). 69/69 integration tests pass. 31 dlsym_passthrough remain (all genuine GCT: resolver DNS 19, stdio vtable 6, dlfcn ext 4, f128 2). Matrix updated: Implemented=2042, GCT=350.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:04:26.919345712Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T18:16:06.943552178Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T18:16:06.943344906Z","close_reason":"Added race-free replacement-guard artifact paths, explicit policy verdict/log fields, and ranked non-threading backlog reporting with passing guard/test verification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","dlfcn","stdio","syscall"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.3","depends_on_id":"bd-24ug","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.3","depends_on_id":"bd-27kh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.3","depends_on_id":"bd-33zg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.3","depends_on_id":"bd-h5x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.3","depends_on_id":"bd-h5x.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1282,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.3","author":"Codex","text":"Codex progress (2026-03-10): nativized two remaining trivial stdio aliases in glibc_internal_abi by routing __fseeko64 -> crate::stdio_abi::fseeko64 and __ftello64 -> crate::stdio_abi::ftello64, eliminating host-glibc passthrough for both symbols. Added focused ABI integration coverage for valid tmpfile seek/tell behavior plus null-stream failure path, and updated support_matrix statuses for both symbols from GlibcCallThrough -> Implemented. Verification in this session: cargo fmt --check PASS; rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test PASS (155/155); rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS; rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings PASS. Full workspace cargo test --all-targets exposed unrelated pre-existing failure in crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/process_abi_test.rs::waitid_with_p_pid (assert waitid should succeed, got -1).","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:11:43Z"},{"id":1283,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.3","author":"Codex","text":"Codex next slice (2026-03-10): continuing the non-threading callthrough burn-down under  by nativizing the  internal resolver-context functions in . Upstream glibc semantics checked: per-thread heap wrapper with refcounted reuse for get/get_preinit, override wrapper for custom ,  preserving errno, and  draining the TLS chain. I’m implementing the minimal local analogue around existing  /  rather than delegating to host glibc.","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:15:46Z"},{"id":1285,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.3","author":"Codex","text":"Codex progress 2026-03-10: continued the non-threading callthrough burn-down with a native resolver-context wave in glibc_internal_abi. The __resolv_context_get family now uses local per-thread heap wrappers instead of RTLD_NEXT host delegation, with put and freeres preserving errno and h_errno. Verification scope stays aligned with the current local resolver model: opaque context lifecycle and non-crashing behavior, not per-context DNS configuration semantics.","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:22:57Z"},{"id":1356,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.3","author":"ChartreuseCrane","text":"ChartreuseCrane: Massive progress on non-threading call-through elimination. Across the session: 79 host delegations eliminated (from ~102 to 23 effective release). Files fully cleared: unistd_abi.rs (57), rpc_abi.rs (6), glibc_internal_abi.rs (1), stdlib_abi.rs (3), resolv_abi.rs (2), setjmp_abi.rs (7, release only). LD_PRELOAD smoke suite now FULLY GREEN: 58/0/6 (was 23/35/6). Remaining 23 release delegations are in io_internal (8), stdio (5), pthread (5, intentional interpose), dlfcn (5, intentional interpose). See epic bd-zh1y for tracking.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:57:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-h5x.4","title":"bd-h5x follow-up: internal errno routing for replacement-guard backlog","description":"Scope: remove host glibc errno TLS accesses from replacement-sensitive ABI code by replacing direct libc::__errno_location usage with FrankenLibC's internal errno ABI accessor or setter where semantics are unchanged. Code review + evidence artifacts present.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","errno","pthread","syscall"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h5x.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h5x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-07T22:53:45.146848841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1412,"issue_id":"bd-h5x.4","author":"Agent1","text":"Agent1 verification (2026-04-15):

## Errno Routing Migration Complete

Commit 57b0825 converted all raw `*crate::errno_abi::__errno_location() = val` patterns to `crate::errno_abi::set_abi_errno(val)` across:
- glibc_internal_abi.rs (102 call sites)
- err_abi.rs (1 call site)
- unistd_abi.rs (1 call site, ftok null pathname guard)

The only remaining libc::__errno_location usage is inside set_abi_errno() itself (errno_abi.rs:53), which intentionally syncs our errno with host libc in interpose mode.

## Verification Commands
- Check no raw errno writes remain: grep returns 0
- set_abi_errno adoptions: 104 in glibc_internal_abi.rs
- errno_abi module tests: 2 passed
- Replacement guard census: call-throughs dropped from 289 to 235

## Delta
- Call-throughs: 289 → 235 (-54 symbols, 18.7% reduction)
- errno_abi now encapsulates all errno writes behind single function

 {"id":"bd-oai","title":"Epic: Dual-Mode Semantics & Deterministic Replay","description":"Dual-mode execution: Strict (default, ABI-compatible, no repair, <20ns) and Hardened (deterministic repair per policy tables, structured JSON evidence per repair, <200ns). Mode selected immutably at process startup for deterministic replay. RuntimeKernelSnapshot (154-field state capture). Evidence symbol records with RaptorQ-inspired redundancy.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Session 30: Epic closed — all children completed

All child beads closed:
- bd-oai.1: Dual-mode selection + strict/hardened execution paths ✅
- bd-oai.2: RuntimeKernelSnapshot (165+ fields) + deterministic replay ✅  
- bd-oai.3: Evidence symbol records + RaptorQ-inspired redundancy ✅

Verification:
- 16 E2E tests in runtime_math_dual_mode_e2e_test.rs (all pass)
- 33 evidence system tests (all pass)
- Deterministic replay produces identical decision streams and JSONL logs
- Branch-diversity enforcement: Healthy/NearViolation/Violation states verified
- Golden snapshot regression gate: JSON equality under deterministic inputs","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:58:48.791246202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T05:13:40.030481360Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T05:13:32Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","dual-mode","epic","frankenlibc","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-oai","depends_on_id":"bd-32e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1124,"issue_id":"bd-oai","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:03Z"}]}

Added helper wiring: load/store/clear thread-local cache; integrated caching into resolved/race/startup branches; preserved strict fallback behavior for unresolved/reentrant paths. Updated tests: ModeStateGuard now snapshots/restores TLS mode cache; cache_reset_reparses_mode_from_environment clears TLS cache; added mode_populates_thread_local_cache_after_resolution.  {"id":"bd-pn6c.3","title":"Investigate and fix the 3 pre-existing pow errno test failures","description":"Three tests have been failing throughout all audit rounds:
- pow_overflow_sets_range_errno
- pow_underflow_sets_range_errno
- pow_zero_negative_sets_range_errno

All expect errno == ERANGE after pow() calls, but errno remains 0. The pow() function correctly calls set_range_errno() which calls set_abi_errno(libc::ERANGE). The test reads errno via __errno_location().

HYPOTHESIS: In test mode, something between set_abi_errno and the test's abi_errno() read is clearing errno. Possible causes:
1. runtime_policy::observe() or decide() inadvertently calls something that sets errno
2. The thread-local errno in test mode is different from the one used by set_abi_errno
3. A Rust standard library operation between the pow call and the assertion resets errno

DEBUG APPROACH: Add eprintln! tracing to set_abi_errno and __errno_location in test mode to verify they use the same TLS slot.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:15.268801545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:27:11.456442127Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:27:11.455469646Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pn6c.3","depends_on_id":"bd-pn6c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:15.268801545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
